data.content:
Implementation review rejected.

Findings
1. `src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts:143-181` only clears `state.resultObserved` for JSON-RPC turns. Copilot follow-up turns go through the plain stdin-stream path, so after `session.task_complete` the runtime stays in `result_observed` even after `sendUserTurn(...)`. I reproduced this directly in the artifact worktree: after emitting `session.task_complete`, calling `sendUserTurn`, and waiting longer than a reduced watchdog timeout, the runtime still reported `resultObserved=true` and `exitReason=result_observed` instead of timing out. This breaks acceptance criterion 3 because follow-up prompting no longer shares the same watchdog/timeout behavior as the other interactive agents.
2. `src/lib/__tests__/copilot-interactive-session.test.ts:238-256` is labeled `resets resultObserved after new turn`, but it never asserts that reset actually happens after `sendUserTurn(...)`, and there is no regression test for a hung follow-up turn timing out. Acceptance criterion 4 is therefore not met for retry/watchdog behavior across follow-up turns.

Validation
- `bun run lint` passed (one existing React Compiler warning in `src/components/beat-form.tsx:368`).
- `bunx tsc --noEmit` passed.
- `bun run test` passed.
- `bun run build` passed.

Recommendation
- Reset the shared runtime state for every successfully sent interactive follow-up turn, not only JSON-RPC turns, and add a regression test that asserts the watchdog can fire after a Copilot follow-up turn hangs.
